grader.method: Independent 16 July 2026 home-page semantic regrade after the marketing-first shared-shell revision; reviewed all five campaign promises, payoffs, arcs, CTAs, evidence boundaries, source cards, and responsive CSS, confirmed unchanged lesson rows remain valid, and verified every rubric floor.

pages.rationale: The home turns consequences, agent construction, and trustworthy evaluation into a coherent seven-part course promise. Seed, support, and constraint language prevents overclaim; two CTAs, a concise evidence boundary, and three claim-linked decision, DQN, and safety sources complete the pitch. E is 14 because the explicit Bellman-target worked trace is no longer rendered on this marketing page.

The page correctly traces action-to-consequence timing, computes return 1.75, distinguishes termination from truncation, separates reward from success metrics and authority, and tests a changed cutoff through committed checks, executable code, diagnostic feedback, and reviewed primary or official sources.

The transition/reward distribution, Markov-sufficiency test, reward-misspecification boundary, and discount arithmetic are consistent: [0,0,10] yields 2.5 at gamma .5 and 8.1 at gamma .9. The runnable notebook uses tolerant floating-point comparison, while changed-state and reward-design tasks require genuine transfer.

The explanation, objective checks, semantic lab, and executable filter all share the same trace: [.6,.4] predicts to [.52,.48] and updates to approximately [.213,.787]. Zero-evidence recovery, calibration limits, committed prediction, and changed-case feedback fully cover belief updating without presenting the fixture as measured performance.

The page correctly computes occupancy-weighted action mass .4×.7=.28, defines how policy, start distribution, dynamics, and discount determine occupancy, and treats zero behavior support as abstention rather than an invented estimate. The supplied occupancy fixture is sufficient for the visible computation objective, and the support/ESS transfer adds appropriate decision depth.

The page supplies a staged working-system build, exact Bellman fixture, changed-slip decision, deterministic starter, hard collision gate, committed checks, retry route, rubric, and a complete five-seed × two-condition reference. The artifact and canonical contract consistently encode slipProbability .1→.3, per-row 1,000-transition budgets, frozen-policy evaluationSuccessRate, its unit and 100-episode aggregation, while preserving the failed constraint row and deterministic-fixture boundary.

Policy evaluation (.596), value iteration (.89), residual reasoning, solver-versus-model validity, and changed-slip transfer are correct across explanation, committed checks, lab, and code. The runnable notebook’s expected observation now gives the exact 1.9933 left output and explains why recurring reward reverses the initially right-favoring backup, making the prediction feedback diagnostic rather than merely confirmatory.

The page correctly computes backward returns [1,2,2], distinguishes first-visit from every-visit aggregation, handles the episode boundary, and separates evaluation episodes from independent training runs. Prediction-gated code and the changed hierarchy case provide specific feedback and an honest uncertainty boundary.

The target 3.7, TD error 1.7, update 2.17, bootstrap ownership, and true-termination versus time-limit-truncation semantics agree across explanation, code, lab, objective checks, and transfer. The underestimation diagnosis and nonlinear/off-policy boundary complete both visible outcomes.

The page correctly identifies ordinary one-step SARSA as on-policy for the behavior policy supplying the next action and tabular Q-learning as off-policy under its usual coverage and step-size assumptions. Targets 3.6 and 5.4, exploratory-policy risk, maximization bias, code, lab, committed checks, and deployment transfer remain technically and pedagogically consistent.

The page specifies one verified interface for planning, Monte Carlo, TD, SARSA, and Q-learning, then stages exact-value checks, ending-mask failure diagnosis, five paired seeds, matched transition budgets, constraints, and a deterministic starter. The canonical artifact explicitly records baseline slip .1, held-out slip .3, environment condition, meanDiscountedReturn, reward-unit semantics, 100-episode aggregation, and per-row 5,000-transition budgets, so the changed-dynamics reference is independently interpretable.

The linear update from [.5,-.25] to [.6,-.05] is correct and exposes why an unsampled neighboring state changes. Executable code, held-out support slices, interference, extrapolation, drifting targets, scale diagnostics, and changed-case transfer fully cover approximation benefits and failure modes.

Replay provenance, target 5.05, online-network comparison 7.3, Polyak update 8.2, batch ownership, freshness-versus-lag trade-off, and reward-change transfer are technically consistent across prose, prediction, code, lab, and locally graded checks.

The page accurately assembles collection, replay, [B,A]→[B] action gathering, no-gradient delayed targets, Huber reduction, online update, target copy, frozen epsilon-zero evaluation, checkpoint ownership, and Q-scale/coverage diagnostics. Its visible scope is assembly and verification of the complete minimal loop and evidence contracts: framework-neutral pseudocode is explicitly labeled, supplies every required external object, and is followed by the implementation capstone rather than being presented as locally executed code.

The page clearly distinguishes epsilon randomness, count bonus .5 versus .1, ensemble disagreement, coverage, calibration, and hard safety shields. The executable count fixture and changed hard-constraint decision require learners to reason about exploration signal, support, and non-compensable risk rather than equating novelty with safety.

The project fully stages DQN micro-tests, replay and target ownership, baseline acceptance, checkpoints, one matched target-interval intervention, five paired seeds, primitive interaction/update budgets, Q-scale gates, retained failures, executable starter checks, and an attempt-gated reference. The artifact names frozen-policy evaluationSuccessRate, successful-episode units, a 100-episode per-seed/interval aggregation, and per-row budgets while honestly remaining a deterministic structure fixture.

The likelihood-ratio loss is correctly computed as about 1.386, the gradient direction is explained, sampled-action and policy-revision ownership are explicit, and the equal-mean/different-variance case forces seed-level reasoning. Code, committed checks, changed-case transfer, and limitations fully support both outcomes.

The backward GAE trace [1.1008,.14,-.5] is correct, and the page distinguishes baseline centering from lambda reach, ending masks, critic leakage, variance, and bias. Executable arithmetic, prediction before reveal, changed-case feedback, and the calibration boundary form a complete learning loop.

The single-policy trace consistently uses old/new sampled probabilities .4/.6, ratio 1.5, clipped surrogate 2.4, entropy from [.6,.4], critic loss .16, and combined loss about -2.3267. Ownership, stop-gradient boundaries, gradient diagnostics, code, lab, and transfer all agree.

The project provides rollout ownership, return/GAE tests, actor and critic updates, PPO diagnostics, KL/finite gates, a paired lambda intervention, five seeds, matched interaction/epoch budgets, starter checks, and an attempt-gated reference. The artifact names mean frozen-policy episodic return, reward-unit semantics, 100-episode aggregation, and per-row budgets while retaining the failed gate and deterministic-fixture evidence boundary.

The page correctly traces state error .4, reward error .3, a missed termination, action sensitivity, and five-step rollout error 2. It distinguishes one-step fit from planning validity and tests model support, compounding error, and predicted-versus-real ranking through executable and changed-case evidence.

The CEM elites and refitted proposal [+1,0] are correct, and 100 candidates × horizon 10 × four iterations gives 4,000 predicted transitions. The page distinguishes receding-horizon feedback from open loop and provides diagnostic transfer for horizon, latency, constraint, and model-error trade-offs.

The real transition, value update, model update, search-control start, imagined transition, provenance label, and imagination-ratio accounting form a complete Dyna loop. Support gating and the matched-model-call transfer keep data efficiency distinct from model-bias amplification.

The uncertainty-penalized scores 9.5 and 9 are correct, while shared ensemble bias, support distance, calibration, abstention, fallback authority, and predicted-versus-real rank reversal are explicitly taught. The learner must commit to a gate decision before seeing the worked reasoning.

The capstone coherently joins learned-model validation, CEM/MPC, an uncertainty guard, matched real steps and model calls, an exploitation injection, fallback, five paired seeds, hard gates, executable starter checks, and a complete horizon comparison. The artifact names constraint-satisfying realized success rate, successful-safe-episode units, 100-episode aggregation, and per-row real/model budgets without presenting its teaching values as measured performance.

Cross-entropy .693, gradient [.5,-.5], the logit update, 90-percent aggregate accuracy with zero turn-state accuracy, episode-split leakage, recovery coverage, and closed-loop covariate shift are accurate and mutually reinforcing across code, lab, checks, and transfer.

The 100+40 row aggregation, expert-label versus executed-action distinction, learner occupancy, query budget, mixture schedule, recovery comparison, and shield trade-off fully teach DAgger. The changed provenance case and error-specific retry prevent superficial recognition.

The page correctly treats counts [100,0] and Q values [5,20] as a zero-support extrapolation requiring constraint, new evidence, a separately validated evaluator, or abstention. The worked trace explicitly states that zero-based action index 1 is the second action, matching the executable support mask, while conservative-learning limits, committed checks, and changed evaluator transfer remain clear.

The page provides exact token positions, [2,6,32] hidden shape, [2,1,6,6] causal mask, shifted action targets, a future-token leakage assertion, support guard, cloning comparison, five seeds, matched logged-transition/update budgets, runnable starter checks, and a complete project rubric. The artifact names support-gated trusted-rollout success rate, successful support-valid episode units, 100-episode aggregation, and per-row budgets with an explicit fixture boundary.

The page correctly preserves five paired effects [5,5,2,3,-54], mean 68.2, median 80, the failed seed, the independent-run hierarchy, matched primitive budgets, and the distinction between AUC and final-window decisions. Local checks and transfer specifically diagnose invalid pooling and post-hoc deletion.

The .05 feasibility rule correctly rejects policy A at cost .12 and accepts B at .03 before reward ranking. Expected versus event-level constraints, shield errors, intervention authority, fallback, recovery, and residual-risk boundaries are all explicit and tested through committed changed cases.

The lesson, public/repository runbooks, external contract, and PyTorch runner align on one-hot [B,5], Q [B,2], smoke seed 11 with 200 steps, 137 matched optimizer updates, eight evaluations, six interval-20 copies and one interval-100 copy, plus full seeds 11/23/41/53/67 at 20,000 steps and 100 evaluations. The runner asserts matched steps/updates and exercised treatment, emits repository and dependency hashes plus resolved accelerator/CUDA/driver provenance, preserves variable outcomes without a promised winner, and gives bounded commands, stop rules, retries, and expected results; external execution remains correctly optional.

The final page stages baseline acceptance, one intervention, complete paired effects, intervals, hard gates, failures, alternatives, a next falsifier, runnable starter checks, and a five-seed × two-arm artifact with matched primitive budgets. Its worked DQN study, project, starter, contract, and artifact now consistently use canonical seeds 11/23/41/53/67, 20,000 interactions, 5,000 updates, and named frozen-policy evaluationSuccessRate with unit and 100-episode aggregation, while retaining null/failure evidence and a bounded fixture claim.
